message:
fix(ft-nn): FocalLoss composes through log_softmax + gather [bd-kxcz]

ft-nn FocalLoss (multi-class softmax variant) used to extract
logits + targets, compute the focal-weighted cross-entropy in
plain f64, and rebuild a requires_grad=false leaf — silently
severing autograd through the logits.

Compose through autograd-aware primitives:
   log_probs = log_softmax(input, last_dim)
   index     = tensor_variable(target as f64, [..., 1], false)
   gathered  = tensor_gather(log_probs, last_dim, index)
   log_pt    = squeeze(gathered, last_dim)
   pt        = exp(log_pt)
   focal     = (1 - pt)^gamma
   per_sample = -alpha * focal * log_pt
   reduce per Reduction (Mean / Sum / None via existing autograd
   reductions; tensor_mean / tensor_sum / per_sample directly)

Same numerical-stability log_softmax form (max-subtract + log1p),
same focal weighting, but the autograd tape now flows through the
input logits.

Second child of frankentorch-cq0b (ft-nn loss-severance umbrella).
Closes frankentorch-kxcz. ft-nn lib tests: 517 passing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

These are critical for PyTorch drop-in parity as every model uses nn.init for weight initialization.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T20:35:09.559619357Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T20:42:44.588973021Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T20:42:44.588716075Z","close_reason":"Implemented: init_constant_, init_zeros_, init_ones_, init_uniform_, init_normal_, init_trunc_normal_, init_eye_, init_xavier_uniform_, init_xavier_normal_, init_kaiming_uniform_, init_kaiming_normal_, init_orthogonal_, init_sparse_, init_dirac_, calculate_gain. All 21 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-kgl","title":"Add explicit oracle-root override to ft-conformance harness config","description":"HarnessConfig currently hardcodes repo_root/legacy_pytorch_code/pytorch. Add an explicit environment override and update the smoke fixture/docs so oracle-path assumptions are transparent and less brittle across worktrees.","notes":"Added FT_LEGACY_ORACLE_ROOT support to HarnessConfig default path resolution, added focused tests for override/fallback behavior, and updated ft-conformance smoke fixture/docs to describe the default local mirror path and explicit override mechanism. Verified with rch-offloaded ft-conformance tests, workspace cargo check, workspace clippy -D warnings, and cargo fmt --check.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:00:53.713746785Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T18:04:52.991856246Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T18:04:52.991244337Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-krf2","title":"RMSNorm in ft-nn severs autograd; transformer training broken","description":"Module RMSNorm at ft-nn/src/lib.rs:4468 implements its forward by extracting tensor_values from both input and weight, computing the normalization manually in plain f64, and rebuilding a requires_grad=false leaf. This silently severs autograd through every RMSNorm layer.

This is critical: RMSNorm is the backbone of LLaMA, Mistral, and most modern transformer architectures. Any model using RMSNorm would have zero gradient on the layers feeding into it (and on the RMSNorm weight itself), making training a no-op.

Fix: compose through autograd-aware primitives:
   reshape input to [batch_dims, norm_dims]
   sq = input * input
   mean_sq = mean(sq, dim=1) → [batch_dims]
   variance = mean_sq + eps
   rms = sqrt(variance)
   rms_broadcast = unsqueeze + expand to [batch_dims, norm_dims]
   normalized = input_reshaped / rms_broadcast
   weight_broadcast = expand weight to [batch_dims, norm_dims]
   weighted = normalized * weight_broadcast
   reshape back to input_shape","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed RMSNorm forward through reshape + mul + mean_dim + sqrt + div primitives — the entire flow is now autograd-aware so gradients flow to both input and weight.

Critical-severity fix: RMSNorm is the backbone of LLaMA / Mistral / most modern transformer architectures. Before this fix, every RMSNorm layer silently severed the autograd tape — meaning any model using RMSNorm couldn't actually train (gradient on parameters feeding into RMSNorm and on the RMSNorm weight itself was always zero).

Math:
   x_flat = reshape(input, [batch_dims, norm_dims])
   sq = x_flat * x_flat
   mean_sq = mean(sq, dim=1)
   rms = sqrt(mean_sq + eps)
   normalized = x_flat / rms (broadcast)
   y = normalized * weight (broadcast)
   reshape back to input_shape

All ops autograd-aware. Added rmsnorm_propagates_gradient_through_input_and_weight regression test (sanity check that gradients are finite and non-zero on input + weight). ft-nn lib tests: 517.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T23:00:43.010190837Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T23:03:15.218216916Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T23:03:15.215855624Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-kxcz","title":"Fix ft-nn FocalLoss autograd via log_softmax + gather composition [child of cq0b]","description":"FocalLoss in ft-nn is the multi-class softmax variant: loss = -alpha * (1 - p_t)^gamma * log(p_t) where p_t = softmax(logits)[target_idx]. Body extracts logits + targets, computes manually, rebuilds non-grad leaf.

Composable through autograd-aware primitives:
   log_probs = log_softmax(logits, last_dim)
   target_idx = tensor_variable(targets, [..., 1], false)   non-grad
   gathered = tensor_gather(log_probs, last_dim, target_idx)
   log_pt = squeeze(gathered, last_dim)
   pt = exp(log_pt)
   focal_factor = (1 - pt)^gamma
   per_sample = -alpha * focal_factor * log_pt
   reduce per Reduction","notes":"Fixed in this commit. Composed ft-nn FocalLoss (multi-class softmax variant) through log_softmax + tensor_gather + autograd primitives. The target-indexed gather replaces the manual offset arithmetic; the rest of the focal weighting (1-pt)^gamma * log_pt composes through tensor_exp + tensor_pow + tensor_mul. ft-nn lib tests: 517.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T23:16:56.557495954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T23:18:15.955248702Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T23:18:15.952864848Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-l8o","title":"Guard cat/index_put shape overflow in ft-kernel-cpu","description":"Use checked shape arithmetic for cat total sizes and index_put stride/value products to avoid usize overflow.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T04:17:00.374296293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T04:17:09.110469945Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T04:17:09.110219010Z","close_reason":"Completed: checked sums/products for cat and index_put","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-lgps","title":"tensor_diagflat severs autograd; delegate to diag_embed via flatten","description":"tensor_diagflat at lib.rs:15978 places a flattened input on the diagonal of a 2-D matrix. tensor_diag_embed (autograd-aware after frankentorch-d180) does exactly this for a 1-D input. So diagflat = diag_embed(flatten(input), offset) and the autograd path is just composition through tensor_reshape + tensor_diag_embed.

Note: diag_embed takes i32 offset; diagflat takes i64. Range check the offset before passing.","notes":"Fixed in this commit. tensor_diagflat now delegates to tensor_reshape + tensor_diag_embed (the latter is autograd-aware after frankentorch-d180). Same observable arithmetic but the autograd tape is preserved. Range-checks the i64 offset for the i32 diag_embed contract. ft-api lib tests: 1171.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T22:47:48.566205395Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T22:49:05.486445868Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T22:49:05.482982898Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-lq1","title":"Fix empty-tensor offset panics in CPU reductions/scans","description":"Audit ft-kernel-cpu reduction/scan/sort/topk paths for empty tensors with non-zero storage_offset causing slice panics; add guards and tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T03:47:18.662219120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T04:09:01.067852162Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T04:09:01.067666560Z","close_reason":"Completed: guard empty-offset paths + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
